description: Re-anchor the harness from the completed `/model` runtime repair to the follow-up task of removing the remaining LiteLLM runtime fallback.
steps: Record in `PROGRESS.md` that the user explicitly requested the runtime path not use LiteLLM because of the reported vulnerability concern.
Replace `PLAN.json` so the active follow-up tracks native provider construction rather than the already-completed `/model list` fix.
Preserve the unrelated `tests/test_coding_mode.py` import failure as an existing repository caveat rather than a new blocker from this provider refactor.

description: Runtime provider creation and model switching no longer rely on `LiteLLMProvider` for openai-compatible backends.
steps: Update `nanobot/providers/factory.py` so the generic fallback constructs native providers instead of `LiteLLMProvider`.
Keep the existing special cases for `aicodewith`, `custom`, `azure_openai`, `anthropic`, `github_copilot`, `openai_oauth`, and `openai_codex`.
Preserve current provider selection semantics while removing the LiteLLM instantiation path.

description: Focused regression coverage proves the runtime factory now uses native providers on the no-LiteLLM path.
steps: Add or update provider-factory tests to assert openai-compatible providers instantiate `OpenAICompatProvider` rather than LiteLLM.
Keep the existing AICodeWith runtime-provider coverage intact.
Avoid relying on stale schema fields while updating the targeted tests.

description: The no-LiteLLM follow-up is verified with focused tests and a real runtime switch check.
steps: Run the focused pytest commands that cover the updated factory path.
Verify a real runtime model switch still routes AICodeWith models correctly after the factory change.
Record the verification results in `PROGRESS.md` before checkpointing.
